Update POLL S.5: live points rate to 0.1 XCN Points per XCN per day

Changed from 1:1 to 0.1 XCN Points per XCN per day for the live system:
- More sustainable rate for mainnet vs. testnet (Poll S.4 stays at 1:1)
- Testnet higher rate (1:1) specifically incentivizes testnet participation
- Live rate (0.1:1) rewards long-term staking without over-inflating points
- Transparent: 100K XCN staked = 10K XCN Points/day, always, no dilution
- Governance vote bonus: 1,000 XCN Points per vote cast

committed gradle.properties. Pin kotlin-gradle-plugin to 1.9.25
directly in build.gradle via sed after prebuild instead.

iOS: Detox requires applesimutils for simulator control but it was
not installed. Add 'brew tap wix/brew && brew install applesimutils'
before the test run step.

commit.message:
feat: Phase 11 metacognition — RAIN.md, relevance gating, deliberate forgetting, gap detection

RAIN.md — Rain's self-knowledge document:
- Created RAIN.md with origin story, agent roster, memory tiers, phase status,
  capabilities, limitations, and key facts — Rain's equivalent of CLAUDE.md
- Loaded at server startup via _load_rain_md() in server.py lifespan
- Injected into every agent's system prompt via _build_runtime_context() so
  Rain can answer self-referential questions from facts, not training guesses
- MultiAgentOrchestrator.__init__ accepts rain_md= parameter

Relevance-gated working memory:
- Tier 2 (working memory) now scoped to current session only via
  get_current_session_messages() — eliminates cross-session contamination
  that was causing "what was my first question?" to pull from prior sessions
- Cross-session relevance handled exclusively by Tier 3 (semantic search),
  which is already relevance-gated via nomic-embed-text cosine similarity
- get_current_session_messages() now includes rowid for pruning

Deliberate forgetting:
- prune_session_memory(keep_recent=20) compresses old session messages into
  a session_facts summary when session exceeds 40 messages, then deletes the
  raw messages — prevents context window from filling with stale exchanges
- Called in a background thread after each response (never blocks pipeline)

Knowledge gap detection (Phase 11):
- knowledge_gaps table created in memory.db
- log_knowledge_gap() + get_top_gaps() added to RainMemory
- After each response where synthesis fired or confidence < 0.72, Reflection
  Agent identifies the specific gap in a background thread and logs it
- Top 3 unresolved gaps surfaced on startup alongside calibration data
- Gaps accumulate over sessions, enabling pattern analysis of systematic
  weaknesses

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Melhora UI de upload com botões separados e mensagens de erro

Upload de Imagem:
- Adiciona dois botões separados: "Tirar Foto" e "Escolher da Galeria"
- Botão de câmera usa capture="environment" para abrir câmera diretamente
- Botão de galeria permite escolher arquivos sem forçar câmera
- Design responsivo: 2 colunas em desktop, 1 em mobile
- Estilo visual diferenciado (azul para câmera, azul claro para galeria)
- Efeito de clique (active:scale-95) para feedback tátil

Tratamento de Erros:
- Detecta erro 404 e informa que servidor está atualizando
- Detecta erro de conexão de rede e informa claramente
- Mensagens específicas para cada tipo de erro HTTP
- Valida resposta JSON antes de processar
- Logs detalhados no console para debugging

UX:
- Mensagens de erro mais claras e acionáveis
- Emoji visual para destacar tipo de erro
- Instruções específicas sobre o que fazer em cada erro

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Fix SVG undefined attrs: replace motion.ellipse/foreignObject with plain SVG

Framer-motion's motion.ellipse and motion.foreignObject can set
cx/cy/x/y to undefined during internal animation setup, causing
SVG parse errors that cascade into React #185. Using plain SVG
elements with static numeric attributes eliminates the issue.
The plane bobbing animation is preserved via motion.div inside
the foreignObject.

commit.message:
perf(v33): Phase 1 — low-hanging fruit globe performance optimizations

- Unify 4x duplicate sun direction calculations into single shared computeSunDirection()
- Eliminate per-frame Date object allocations (4x new Date() → Date.now() cached)
- Reduce interaction sphere segments 64x64 → 16x16 (invisible mesh, -93% triangles)
- Reduce atmosphere segments 64x64 → 32x32
- Tune Bloom: threshold 0.4→0.7, intensity 1.2→0.8, radius 0.75→0.5
- Fix AtmosphereGlow inline uniforms → useMemo pre-creation
- Replace per-frame Vector3/Quaternion allocations with module-scope temps
- Replace clone().multiplyScalar → copy().multiplyScalar in GlobeEventPulse
- Replace Date.now() with R3F clock-based timing in GlobeTradeRoutes
- Add EMPTY_DOM_MAP/EMPTY_STATE_MAP/EMPTY_SET constants to prevent re-renders

commit.message:
treatise: Ch4 companion v1.5.0 — Layer 2 attribution corrected

Bishop adversarial review (second pass) identified a structural slip:
Layer 2 was using Parfitian vocabulary for what are actually contractualist
requirements.

Changes:
- Layer 2: Parfit establishes the *standard* (formal succession insufficient,
  psychological engagement required — same insight as body-continuity in
  personal identity). The obligation-generating *mechanism* is Scanlonian:
  genuine engagement constitutes entering a normative arrangement that the
  prior parties could not reasonably reject. Successor forms new psychological
  connections *about* prior commitments (second-order beliefs), not continuity
  *with* the prior Relation R chain. Attribution corrected accordingly.
- Genuine engagement: knowledge requirement re-attributed from Parfit to
  Scanlon. Relation R holds regardless of reflective access (deep sleep
  doesn't interrupt it). The epistemic condition is a normative-fairness
  point: you can't be held to commitments you had no access to.
- Successor's Paradox: mechanism language updated. Parfit explains why the
  paradox arises; Scanlon explains the resolution mechanism.

Architecture unchanged. Governance prescription unchanged. Attributions
are now precise.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
